w:r.w:t: Complete Problem Diagnosis + Algorithm Fix Guide
Maine teri saari 19 screenshots ache se dekhi. Yeh 7 exact problems hain jo clearly visible hain:
Img 12: "Kitchen floating (no wall connection)" — Validation ka error. Rooms GLB mesh mein wall se connect nahi hain.
Img 4,5,6: 3D view mein rooms ke beech pink/red overlapping lines visible hain. Floor slab aur room mesh same Y-position par hain — Z-fighting.
Img 1,2,3: Floor 1 map mein rooms ek doosre ke upar hain, aur Floor 2 mein bahut saare rooms plot ke andar nahi hain.
Img 18,19: Prompt mein "2 bedroom" bola, Room Match Matrix mein Bedroom: Req 0 | Built 0 — parser correctly nahi detect kar raha.
Img 9: Floor 2 mein sirf 3 rooms hain — Bathroom, Store, Balcony. Floor 1 par bhi sirf Bedroom + Kitchen. Distribution logic uneven hai.
Img 5,6: GLB models (bed, toilet, etc.) visible hain but size aur position off hain. GLB default scale != room scale.
Img 5,6: Staircase physically between floors but not connecting them in 3D. No landing geometry, no floor hole opening.
2. Root Cause Analysis — Kyun Ho Raha Hai
Tera floor slab mesh aur room floor mesh SAME Y coordinate par hain.
Three.js mein jab 2 faces same position par hon, renderer alternately dono ko dikhata hai — yahi 'flickering red lines' hain.
Fix: Floor slab ko Y=0 pe rakho, room mesh ko Y=0.001 (1mm upar) pe.
Tera current placement engine rooms ko independently place kar raha hai.
Koi AABB (Axis-Aligned Bounding Box) check nahi hai — isliye rooms ek doosre ke andar ghus jaate hain.
Floating rooms isliye hain kyunki Vastu zone scan corner se shuru karta hai, aur overlap check fail ho jaata hai.
// ✅ AABB Collision Check — apne placement engine mein add karo
const GAP = 0.3; // wall thickness in feet
for (let z = 0; z <= plotD - room.d; z += 1) {
for (let x = 0; x <= plotW - room.w; x += 1) {
Img 18/19 mein: "mujhe 2000 square fir ka ghar chaiye jis mai 2 floor ho or 1 floor par kitch, living room, pooja room, staris and 2 floor par let bat, 2 bed room, boucany and kitch ho"
Parser ne 'bat' ko bath nahi maana — keyword map incomplete hai.
"boucany" ko balcony nahi maana — fuzzy matching nahi hai.
BHK + floor-wise assignment SAME pipeline mein mix ho raha hai.
Tera code rooms ko round-robin distribute karta hai: room[i % floors].
Isse bedroom ground floor pe bhi ja sakta hai, living room upper floor pe.
Floor budget (max area per floor) enforce nahi ho raha.
3. Complete Fix Algorithm — Step by Step
STEP 1 — Single Source of Truth: RoomSpec[]
Saari planning ke liye ek hi data structure use karo. Yeh
type apne project mein define karo:
x: number; z: number; w: number; d: number;
// CRITICAL: 2D map aur 3D view SAME x,z use karein
// 2D SVG: rect at (x * SVG_SCALE, z * SVG_SCALE)
// 3D mesh: position.set(x * M_PER_FT, y3d, z * M_PER_FT)
Tera current code sab kuch ek saath karta hai. Inhe 5 alag phases mein tod:
function parsePrompt(text: string): { counts: Record<string,number>, floors: number } {
if (/duplex|double|do floor|2 floor/.test(lo)) floors = Math.max(2, floors);
// Step D: Keyword scan with fuzzy
const COVERAGE = 0.68; // 68% usable after setbacks + walls
function calcBudget(plotW: number, plotD: number, floors: number) {
'bathroom': -1, // attach to floor with most private rooms
// Bathroom: go where most private rooms are
target = used[0] + room.area <= budget ? 0 : bestFloor(used, room.area, budget);
target = used[upper] + room.area <= budget ? upper : bestFloor(used, room.area, budget);
b.push({ ...s, id: s.id + '_f' + i, floorIdx: i })
Phase 4: Place (Vastu + AABB — No Gaps, No Overlaps)
x < o.x + o.w + WALL && x + w > o.x - WALL &&
z < o.z + o.d + WALL && z + d > o.z - WALL
function tryZone(xMin, xMax, zMin, zMax, w, d) {
for (let z = zMin; z <= zMax - d; z++) {
for (let x = xMin; x <= xMax - w; x++) {
if (x + w <= plotW && z + d <= plotD && !overlaps(x, z, w, d))
for (let z = 0; z <= plotD - d; z++) {
for (let x = 0; x <= plotW - w; x++) {
if (!overlaps(x, z, w, d)) return { x, z, vastuOk: false };
if (!pos) { console.warn(room.label + ' does not fit!'); continue; }
const placed_room = { ...room, x: pos.x, z: pos.z,
occ.push({ x: pos.x, z: pos.z, w: room.w, d: room.d });
const rect = createSVGRect(r.x * SVG_SC, r.z * SVG_SC, r.w * SVG_SC, r.d * SVG_SC);
// NO offset needed — same coordinate system
const geometry = new THREE.BoxGeometry(r.w * FT, r.h * FT, r.d * FT);
// Center the box: Three.js BoxGeometry is centered at origin
r.y3d * FT + (r.h * FT) / 2, // Y center (above floor)
Three.js mein jab 2 meshes same plane par hote hain, renderer decide nahi kar paata kaun aage hai.
Flickering red/pink lines isi se aate hain.
// GLB models default scale != room scale
// Fix: normalize GLB to 1x1x1 unit box, then scale to room
// Step 1: Get bounding box of model
// Step 2: Scale to fit room (80% of room size)
function createStaircase(x, z, w, d, floorIdx, floorH) {
const geo = new THREE.BoxGeometry(w * FT * 0.9, stepH, stepD);
mesh.position.set((x + w/2)*FT, baseY + i*stepH + stepH/2, (z + i*stepD/FT)*FT);
mesh.position.set((x + w/2)*FT, baseY + i*stepH + stepH/2, (z + d/2*FT - j*stepD)*FT);
// X-Ray mode: make walls transparent to see inside
5. Room Validation Engine — 'Needs Fix' Ko Fix Karo
Image 12 mein tera validation engine 'Needs Fix' show kar raha hai. Yeh ek proper validation system hai:
function validateLayout(rooms: RoomSpec[], plotW: number, plotD: number): ValidationResult {
for (let j = i + 1; j < rooms.length; j++) {
const ox = a.x < b.x+b.w && a.x+a.w > b.x;
const oz = a.z < b.z+b.d && a.z+a.d > b.z;
if (r.x + r.w > plotW || r.z + r.d > plotD)
const dist = Math.hypot(kitchen.x - living.x, kitchen.z - living.z);
if (dist > 20) warnings.push('Kitchen + Living Room too far apart');
return { valid: errors.length === 0, errors, warnings, openAreas: [] };
Add polygonOffset to floor material (Z-fighting fix)
Add AABB overlap check to placement engine
GLB scale normalize to room bounding box
Smart floor distribution: ground=common, upper=private rooms
Staircase dog-leg geometry with floor opening
Room Match Matrix: show Req vs Applied vs Built clearly
Vastu score meter: calculate based on placed rooms vs ideal positions
7. Complete Data Flow — Sab Kaise Connect Hota Hai
│ → feasible? show green banner : red banner │

5 phases ko alag rakho. Sab phases ek hi RoomSpec[] data structure use karein. 2D aur 3D ke liye same x,z coordinates use karo — sirf rendering alag karo. Z-fighting ke liye polygonOffset + y3d offset. AABB check placement se pehle. Floor distribution: ground=common, upper=private.
BricksBazar AI Architect — Algorithm Fix Guide v4.0 | March 2026
